title: NHS England Introduces New Rules to Improve Patient Communication
subtitle: New standards aim to end the frustration of missed appointments and poor updates for millions on waiting lists.

summary.en: NHS England is introducing new rules to improve how hospitals communicate with patients. Patients will now receive at least three weeks' notice before their hospital appointments or operations. This change follows reports that many people were not receiving information until after their appointment dates had passed. The health service is looking to modern businesses for inspiration on how to provide better customer service. These updates will be managed through the NHS app, as well as by letter or text for those who prefer it.
summary.zh: 英國國家醫療保健服務正在引進新規則，以改善醫院與病患之間的溝通方式。病患現在於醫院預約或手術前，至少會收到三週的通知。此項變革源於多項報告指出，許多民眾在預約日期過後才收到通知。醫療服務單位正向現代企業取經，學習如何提供更優質的客戶服務。這些更新將透過英國國家醫療保健服務的應用程式進行管理，偏好信件或簡訊的民眾亦可透過上述方式接收通知。

paragraphs.en: Millions of people waiting for hospital treatment in England will soon receive better communication regarding their care. NHS England has announced a new plan to ensure that patients are given at least three weeks' notice before any operation, diagnostic test, or meeting with a specialist. This move is part of a wider effort to improve the experience of patients who are currently waiting for medical help.
paragraphs.zh: 在英格蘭，數百萬名等待醫院治療的民眾，很快就能在醫療照護的溝通上獲得改善。英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS England）宣布了一項新計畫，確保病患在進行任何手術、診斷檢查或專科醫師會診前，至少會收到三週的通知。此舉是改善目前等待醫療協助之病患就醫體驗的廣泛計畫之一。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, the new policy is designed to stop the common problem of appointment letters arriving after the scheduled time has already passed. Research by the King’s Fund, a health thinktank, found that nearly one in four patients were notified about their appointments too late. Many patients have reported feeling "left in the dark" about their health status after being placed on a waiting list, which often leads to unnecessary worry and confusion.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，這項新政策旨在解決預約通知信件在預約時間過後才送達的常見問題。健康智庫「國王基金會」（King’s Fund）的研究發現，近四分之一的病患收到預約通知的時間太晚。許多病患表示，在被列入等待名單後，對於自己的健康狀況感到「被蒙在鼓裡」，這往往導致不必要的擔憂與困惑。

paragraphs.en: Jim Mackey, the chief executive of NHS England, described the current way hospitals communicate with patients as "clearly unacceptable." He noted that navigating the health system can feel like "walking through treacle," a phrase used to describe a process that is slow, difficult, and frustrating. To fix these issues, NHS England has introduced eight new "patient experience standards." These rules aim to provide what officials call "five-star customer service."
paragraphs.zh: 英國國家醫療保健服務執行長麥基（Jim Mackey）形容目前醫院與病患溝通的方式「顯然無法接受」。他指出，在醫療體系中摸索就像「在糖漿中行走」一樣，用來形容過程緩慢、困難且令人沮喪。為了修正這些問題，英國國家醫療保健服務推出了八項新的「病患體驗標準」。這些規則旨在提供官員所稱的「五星級客戶服務」。

paragraphs.en: To develop these new standards, NHS leaders looked at how successful private companies, such as Amazon and John Lewis, keep their customers updated. The goal is to make sure that patients receive clear information from the moment their GP (a general practitioner, or family doctor) refers them to a hospital. In the future, patients will be able to use the NHS app to see when a specialist has accepted their referral and confirm they are officially on the waiting list. For those who do not use the app, hospitals will continue to send updates via letter or text message.
paragraphs.zh: 為了制定這些新標準，英國國家醫療保健服務的領導階層參考了亞馬遜（Amazon）與約翰路易斯（John Lewis）等成功私人企業如何更新客戶資訊的做法。目標是確保病患從家醫科醫師（GP）轉診至醫院的那一刻起，就能收到明確的資訊。未來，病患將能使用英國國家醫療保健服務的應用程式，查看專科醫師何時接受轉診，並確認自己已正式列入等待名單。對於不使用應用程式的民眾，醫院將繼續透過信件或簡訊發送更新資訊。

paragraphs.en: Currently, about 6 million people in England are waiting for roughly 7 million different tests, operations, and appointments. The sheer size of this waiting list has made communication a major challenge for the 205 NHS trusts across the country. By requiring hospitals to provide at least three weeks' notice, officials hope to reduce the number of missed appointments and help patients plan their lives more effectively while they wait for care.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，英格蘭約有六百萬人正在等待約七百萬項不同的檢查、手術與預約。這份等待名單的龐大規模，使得溝通成為全國兩百零五個醫療信託機構面臨的一大挑戰。透過要求醫院至少提前三週通知，官員希望減少錯過預約的情況，並協助病患在等待照護期間能更有效地規劃生活。

paragraphs.en: Patient advocacy groups have welcomed the change. William Pett, who works for Healthwatch England—an organization that represents the views of patients—said the new standards are a positive step. He noted that for too long, patients have complained about missing letters, delayed information, or feeling completely forgotten by the system for months at a time. According to Pett, these new rules send a clear signal that the quality of communication is just as important as the length of time a patient has to wait for treatment.
paragraphs.zh: 病患權益倡議團體對此項變革表示歡迎。代表病患意見的組織「英格蘭健康觀察」（Healthwatch England）成員佩特（William Pett）表示，這些新標準是正面的一步。他指出，長期以來，病患不斷抱怨信件遺失、資訊延誤，或感覺自己被醫療體系遺忘數月之久。根據佩特的說法，這些新規則傳達了一個明確的訊號：溝通品質與病患等待治療的時間長度同樣重要。

paragraphs.en: While the plan focuses on communication, it also highlights the ongoing pressure on the NHS to manage its large waiting lists. Officials stated that the new standards are not just about sending letters on time; they are about fixing the basics of how the health service treats people. By making information easier to access, the NHS hopes to reduce the need for patients to call hospitals multiple times just to find out the status of their care.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然該計畫聚焦於溝通，但也凸顯了英國國家醫療保健服務在管理龐大等待名單上所面臨的持續壓力。官員表示，新標準不僅是為了準時寄送信件，更是為了改善醫療服務對待民眾的基本方式。透過讓資訊更容易取得，英國國家醫療保健服務希望減少病患為了查詢照護進度而多次致電醫院的需求。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the success of this plan will depend on how quickly England’s 205 NHS trusts can update their internal systems. Jim Mackey has instructed hospitals to implement these changes as soon as possible. As the health service continues to work through high demand, these new standards are intended to bring more clarity and fairness to the patient experience, ensuring that people are no longer left wondering when they will finally receive the medical attention they need.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，此計畫的成功將取決於英格蘭兩百零五個醫療信託機構更新內部系統的速度。麥基已指示醫院盡快落實這些變革。隨著醫療服務持續應對高需求，這些新標準旨在為病患體驗帶來更多清晰度與公平性，確保民眾不再需要為了何時能獲得所需的醫療照護而感到茫然。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be kept uninformed about something; to not know what is happening.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 被蒙在鼓裡；對情況一無所知。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思是「留在黑暗中」，這裡用來形容病人對自己的健康狀況毫無頭緒。文中：Many patients have reported feeling "left in the dark" about their health status after being placed on a waiting list, which often leads to unnecessary worry and confusion.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To describe a process that is extremely slow, difficult, and frustrating.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 形容過程極其緩慢、困難且令人沮喪。
keyTerms.noteZh: Treacle 是糖漿，在糖漿中行走會非常費力，用來比喻官僚體系的低效率。文中：He noted that navigating the health system can feel like "walking through treacle," a phrase used to describe a process that is slow, difficult, and frustrating.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To send someone to a specialist or a different department for help.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 轉診；將某人轉介給專家或相關單位。
keyTerms.noteZh: 在醫療語境中，指家庭醫生將病人轉介給專科醫生。文中：The goal is to make sure that patients receive clear information from the moment their GP (a general practitioner, or family doctor) refers them to a hospital.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: People awaiting hospital treatment to get three weeks’ notice under NHS England plans

quiz.question: What is the primary requirement for hospitals regarding patient notifications under the new NHS plan?

options.A: They must provide all medical results via the NHS app.
options.B: They must give patients at least three weeks' notice before any appointment or procedure.
options.C: They must send a confirmation letter within 24 hours of a GP referral.
options.D: They must reduce the total waiting list to under 6 million people.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段與第五段，NHS 新規定明確要求醫院必須在手術、診斷測試或專科會診前，至少提前三週通知病患。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到 App 是選項之一，而非唯一途徑。選項 C 為文章未提及的細節。選項 D 雖然提到 600 萬人，但那是目前的等待人數，而非新政策的規定目標。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might the new communication standards lead to a decrease in the number of calls hospitals receive?

options.A: Because patients will be able to perform their own diagnostic tests at home.
options.B: Because the NHS will hire more staff to answer phones.
options.C: Because patients will have easier access to information about their care status.
options.D: Because the total number of people waiting for treatment will decrease significantly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第七段，文章提到透過讓資訊更易於取得（making information easier to access），NHS 希望減少病患因查詢醫療進度而多次致電醫院的需求。選項 A 與事實不符；選項 B 文章並未提及增加接線人員；選項 D 雖然是長期目標，但與減少電話詢問的直接因果關係較弱，且短期內等待人數不會立即大幅下降。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the phrase 'walking through treacle' imply about the NHS system?

options.A: That it is highly efficient and moves quickly.
options.B: That it is overly expensive for the average patient.
options.C: That it is slow, difficult, and frustrating to navigate.
options.D: That it is dangerous and requires extreme caution.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第三段明確解釋了這個片語是用來形容一個過程「緩慢、困難且令人沮喪」（slow, difficult, and frustrating）。treacle（糖漿）質地黏稠，在其中行走會非常費力，因此用來比喻官僚體系運作緩慢。選項 A 與原意相反；選項 B 與 D 雖然在現實中可能存在，但並非該隱喻在此處的語境含義。

quiz.question: What is the central purpose of the new NHS communication plan?

options.A: To replace all traditional paper-based communication with digital technology.
options.B: To improve patient experience by standardizing and clarifying communication regarding care.
options.C: To blame private companies for the current failures of the public health system.
options.D: To increase the number of specialists available for diagnostic tests.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。全文的核心在於 NHS 為了改善病患在等待期間的體驗，透過建立新的「病患體驗標準」（patient experience standards），確保資訊透明且及時。選項 A 不正確，因為醫院仍會使用信件或簡訊；選項 C 錯誤，文章引用私人企業是為了學習其成功經驗，而非指責；選項 D 並非該計畫的主要目標，計畫重點在於「溝通」而非增加醫療人力。
